Use the workflow-generated flaky E2E context in `{{FLAKY_CONTEXT_PATH}}` and the human-readable summary in `{{FLAKY_SUMMARY_PATH}}` to identify and fix flaky E2E tests.

Requirements:
1. Work on the existing branch `{{BRANCH_NAME}}`.
2. Treat `{{FLAKY_CONTEXT_PATH}}` and `{{FLAKY_SUMMARY_PATH}}` as the authoritative flaky-input set for this run. You may use `gh` for **read-only** operations (e.g. fetching action logs, viewing workflow runs) but do not use it to write or mutate any GitHub state.
3. If the workflow-generated context reports no flaky tests worth fixing, leave the repository unchanged and do not write PR metadata.
4. If you make a fix, run the most relevant targeted E2E verification for the touched spec files and create a local git commit using a Conventional Commit message.
5. If you make a fix, write PR metadata to `{{PR_METADATA_PATH}}` as exactly one JSON object with this shape: `{"title":"Conventional Commit style title","summary":"short purpose-driven summary","rationale":"reviewer context"}`.
6. Do not push, and do not open or edit pull requests.
